rulings.Case Title: HOWARD WEISS VS. SHANNON WILLS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 13, 2013, line 3, DEFENDANT MARIN EXPERIMENTAL TEACHING TRAINING AND ADVISING's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Defendant shifted the burden but Plaintiff has raised a triable issue regarding whether Defendant Shannon Wills was acting within the course and scope of her employment at the time the accident occurred. See Watson Declaration in Opposition to Motion, Ex. 3 ("Wills Declaration"). Wills was the most authoritative staff person at METTA. (Wills Declaration 9.) METTA had a "METTA Mentors" program. (Id. 10.) Wills' job duties included helping the mentees in community building, helping the mentees in broadening their base of support and helping them in explorations of meditation. (Id. 12-14.) On the night of the collision, Wills was with mentees who were participating in the program. Wills and the "METTA mentees" had been together at a meditation gathering in Santa Clara hosted by Mr. Mehta. Wills thought Mr. Mehta's work was compatible with and relevant to nonviolence work, and thought it was appropriate for mentees to be exposed to his work and ideas. (Id. 21.) Wills and the mentees then viewed the City lights together as part of that activity, to reflect on it, and to strengthen bonds. (Id.) Wills stated that having the mentees with her in her vehicle at the time of the collision was an understandable exercise of her discretion and duties to engage in community building, broaden mentees' exposures to the nonviolence community, and provide mentees with exposure to the real world of non-violence. (Id. 24.) D'Amico and its progeny do not apply here. "In a nutshell, the rule bars a party opposing summary judgment from filing a declaration that purports to impeach his or her own prior sworn testimony." Scalf v. D.B. Log Homes, Inc. (2005) 128 Cal. App. 4th 1510, 1522. Wills is not a party opposing summary judgment. In any event, her declaration is not directly contradictory of her responses to the requests for admission. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TELEGRAPH HILL PROPERTIES, INC., VS. PHOENIX RABBIT, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILIT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Deposition And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 13, 2013, Line 4. Defendant PHOENIX RABBIT, LLC'S, a California limited liability company, Motion to Compel Deposition and for Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel deposition is granted. WB Coyle is ordered to appear for deposition on June 4, 2013 at the offices of Seiler, Epstein, Zigler & Applegate at 9:30 a.m. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IVAN D'ORSO, INDIVIDUALLY AND GUARDIAN AD LITEM FO VS. ATHAVAN OTTO THAV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Reclassify Case From The Unlimited Jurisdiction Court To The Limited Jurisdiction Court
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 13, 2013, line 8, DEFENDANT ATHAVAN THAV's motion To Reclassify Case From The Unlimited Jurisdiction Court To The Limited Jurisdiction Court. The motion is denied. First, defendant fails to explain why the motion could not have been filed earlier and fails to comply with CCP 403.040(b)(2). Second, defendant fails to demonstrate that recovery over $25,000 is virtually unattainable. See Walker v. Sup. Crt. (1991) 53 Cal.3d 257, 269. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 13, 2013, line 11, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with 15 days leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GREG CONTOS, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS VS. KOKKARI LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Pltf'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories, First Set Of Inspection Demands And Req, For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 13, 2013, Line 6. Plaintiff GREG CONTOS', on behalf of himself and all others similarly situated, Motion to Compel Further Discovery Responses to Plaintiff's First Set of Special Interrogatories, First Set of Inspection Demands, and Request for Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties are to appear. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MANUEL AUSTIN et al VS. MARK ALEX BOLLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 13, 2013, line 14, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege additional facts and a statutory basis to establish government tort liability against CCSF for the first cause of action for negligence and second cause of action for premises liability. (Gov. Code § 815.) The court declines to rule on the sufficiency of the allegations of the proposed First Amended Complaint as this pleading is outside the scope of the instant motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CITIBANK N.A., A NATIONAL BANKING ASSOCIATION VS. LUCAS O. STICKNEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 13, 2013, line 15, PLAINTIFF CITIBANK N.A., A NATIONAL BANKING ASSOCIATION's Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment. Denied. Plaintiff does not identify the property sought to be attached. (CCP § 484.020(e).) At Item 9c, Plaintiff states that the Property subject to attachment is specified in an attached Schedule. No schedule is attached to the application. Plaintiff has not established the probability of its claim. (CCP § 484.090(a)(2).) Plaintiff proffers the declaration of Michael Gesimondo in support of the application. In his declaration, Mr. Gesimondo refers to Exhibits 1 - 5, which include the Corporation Resolution, Business Loan Agreement, U.S. Small Business Administration Note, Unconditional Guarantee, and loan summary and transaction printouts. These exhibits are not attached to his declaration. Lastly, to the extent that Plaintiff seeks to attach real property located at 630 Natoma Street, Plaintiff has not presented any evidence to show that this property is held in a revocable trust. (Prob. Code § 18200.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

Otherwise, the matter will be continued to May 24, 2013 and moving party must send notice to all parties of the new hearing date. =(302/MJM)
